sn12.63:1.2: “There are these four nutriments for the maintenance of beings who have come into being or for the support of those in search of a place to be born.
sn12.63:1.4: Physical food, gross or refined; contact as the second, intellectual intention the third, and consciousness the fourth.
sn12.63:1.5: These are the four nutriments for the maintenance of beings who have come into being or for the support of those in search of a place to be born.
sn12.63:2.1: “And how is physical food to be regarded?
sn12.63:2.2: Suppose a couple, husband & wife, taking meager provisions, were to travel through a desert.
sn12.63:2.3: With them would be their only baby son, loved & endearing.
sn12.63:2.4: Then the meager provisions of the couple going through the desert would be used up & depleted
sn12.63:2.5: while there was still a stretch of the desert yet to be crossed.
sn12.63:2.6: The thought would occur to them,
sn12.63:2.7: ‘Our meager provisions are used up & depleted
sn12.63:2.8: while there is still a stretch of this desert yet to be crossed.
sn12.63:2.9: What if we were to kill this only baby son of ours, loved & endearing, and make dried meat & jerky? That way—chewing on the flesh of our son—at least the two of us would make it through this desert.
sn12.63:2.10: Otherwise, all three of us would perish.’ So they would kill their only baby son, loved & endearing, and make dried meat & jerky. Chewing on the flesh of their son, they would make it through the desert.
sn12.63:2.11: While eating the flesh of their only son, they would beat their breasts, (crying,)
sn12.63:2.12: ‘Where have you gone, our only baby son? Where have you gone, our only baby son?’
sn12.63:3.1: Now what do you think, monks?
sn12.63:3.2: Would that couple eat that food playfully or for intoxication, or for putting on bulk, or for beautification?”
sn12.63:3.4: “Wouldn’t they eat that food simply for the sake of making it through that desert?”
sn12.63:3.6: “In the same way, I tell you, is the nutriment of physical food to be regarded.
sn12.63:3.7: When physical food is comprehended, passion for the five strings of sensuality is comprehended.
sn12.63:3.8: When passion for the five strings of sensuality is comprehended, there is no fetter bound by which a disciple of the noble ones would come back again to this world.
sn12.63:4.1: “And how is the nutriment of contact to be regarded?
sn12.63:4.2: Suppose a flayed cow were to stand leaning against a wall. The creatures living in the wall would chew on it.
sn12.63:4.3: If it were to stand leaning against a tree, the creatures living in the tree would chew on it.
sn12.63:4.4: If it were to stand exposed to water, the creatures living in the water would chew on it.
sn12.63:4.5: If it were to stand exposed to the air, the creatures living in the air would chew on it.
sn12.63:4.6: For wherever the flayed cow were to stand exposed, the creatures living there would chew on it.
sn12.63:4.7: In the same way, I tell you, is the nutriment of contact to be regarded.
sn12.63:4.8: When the nutriment of contact is comprehended, the three feelings [pleasure, pain, neither pleasure nor pain] are comprehended.
sn12.63:4.9: When the three feelings are comprehended, I tell you, there is nothing further for a disciple of the noble ones to do.
sn12.63:5.1: “And how is the nutriment of intellectual intention to be regarded?
sn12.63:5.2: Suppose there were a pit of glowing embers, deeper than a man’s height, full of embers that were neither flaming nor smoking,
sn12.63:5.3: and a man were to come along—loving life, hating death, loving pleasure, abhorring pain—
sn12.63:5.4: and two strong men, having grabbed him by the arms, were to drag him to the pit of embers.
sn12.63:5.5: To get far away would be that man’s intention, far away would be his wish, far away would be his aspiration.
sn12.63:5.8: ‘If I fall into this pit of glowing embers, I will meet with death from that cause, or with death-like pain.’
sn12.63:5.9: In the same way, I tell you, is the nutriment of intellectual intention to be regarded.
sn12.63:5.10: When the nutriment of intellectual intention is comprehended, the three forms of craving [for sensuality, for becoming, and for non-becoming] are comprehended.
sn12.63:5.11: When the three forms of craving are comprehended, I tell you, there is nothing further for a disciple of the noble ones to do.
sn12.63:6.1: “And how is the nutriment of consciousness to be regarded?
sn12.63:6.2: Suppose that, having arrested a thief, a criminal, they were to show him to the king:
sn12.63:6.3: ‘This is a thief, a criminal for you, your majesty. Impose on him whatever punishment you like.’
sn12.63:6.5: ‘Go, men, and stab him in the morning with a hundred spears.’
sn12.63:6.6: So they would stab him in the morning with a hundred spears.
sn12.63:6.7: Then the king would say at noon,
sn12.63:6.11: ‘Go, men, and stab him at noon with a hundred spears.’
sn12.63:6.12: So they would stab him at noon with a hundred spears.
sn12.63:6.13: Then the king would say in the evening,
sn12.63:6.17: ‘Go, men, and stab him in the evening with a hundred spears.’
sn12.63:6.18: So they would stab him in the evening with a hundred spears.
sn12.63:6.19: Now what do you think, monks?
sn12.63:6.20: Would that man, being stabbed with three hundred spears a day, experience pain & distress from that cause?”
sn12.63:6.21: “Even if he were to be stabbed with only one spear, lord, he would experience pain & distress from that cause,
sn12.63:6.22: to say nothing of three hundred spears.”
sn12.63:6.23: “In the same way, I tell you, monks, is the nutriment of consciousness to be regarded.
sn12.63:6.24: When the nutriment of consciousness is comprehended, name-&-form is comprehended. When name-&-form is comprehended, I tell you, there is nothing further for a disciple of the noble ones to do.”
